hero.description: WebSkill turns the browser into a smart Agent execution environment. It completely solves the LLM context bloating issue, ensures absolute privacy through OPFS and Worker sandboxes, and transforms Agent capabilities into lightweight, dynamically distributed SaaS.

home.agentSkillDesc: An Agent Skill is a modular, reusable, standardized capability unit. It provides domain-specific knowledge and strict execution workflows to the Large Language Model.

agentSkillScenarios.title: Development & Code
agentSkillScenarios.desc: Code review standards, TDD workflows, complex Git branch merging.

agentSkillScenarios.title: Business & Docs
agentSkillScenarios.desc: Applying company templates, brand colors, financial analysis, multi-format report generation.

agentSkillScenarios.title: Security & Compliance
agentSkillScenarios.desc: Compliance checks, penetration testing rules, and vulnerability scanning workflows.

problemsSolved.title: Mitigates Context Bloating
problemsSolved.desc: Avoids overloading the LLM with dozens of pages of background context, reducing API costs and hallucinations.

problemsSolved.title: Bridges Domain Expertise
problemsSolved.desc: Codifies human expert experience into the model so it understands internal company strategies and processes.

problemsSolved.title: Eliminates Repetitive Prompts
problemsSolved.desc: Creates prompts once for lifetime reuse, standardizing workflows and ensuring consistent actions.

home.webskillDesc: Web-Based Agent Skill (WebSkill) is a modular, reusable, standardized capability unit that runs completely within the browser. It does not rely on traditional backend services.

webskillFeatures.title: Browser-Native Closed Loop
webskillFeatures.desc: Completes the workflow directly in the browser, eliminating large data transmission overhead to the backend.

webskillFeatures.title: Absolute Privacy Isolation
webskillFeatures.desc: Stores files in OPFS and runs scripts in Web Worker sandboxes, ensuring local data never leaves the device.

webskillFeatures.title: Dynamic Personal Assets
webskillFeatures.desc: Evolves based on user habits, creating new SKILL files locally to personalize the AI experience.

home.trinityDesc: A user-centric closed loop powered by LLM, WebSkill, WebMCP, and Generative UI.
home.trinityLLM: LLM acts as the routing engine, reasoning and scheduling skills based on user intent.
home.trinityWebSkill: Solves the 'when to call which tool' problem and prevents context explosion from loading too many MCP tools.
home.trinityGenerativeUI: Solves missing parameter interactions during WebSkill execution and renders data into UI after task completion.
home.trinityWebMCP: The frontend native executor that operates DOM nodes or sends backend requests.
home.comparisonDesc: WebSkill eliminates the need for complex backend deployments and data synchronization, providing a much safer, faster, and personalized experience.

compRows.webskill: Closed loop in browser, zero data egress
Direct DOM operation, session state access
Distributed with Web App, zero install

home.diagramAnnotation1: WebSkill solves the 'when to call tools' problem for LLMs, achieving a closed-loop frontend Agent task.
home.diagramAnnotation2: WebSkill prevents context explosion from massive MCP tool loads.
home.diagramAnnotation3: Generative UI solves the human-computer interaction problem of missing WebSkill parameters and result rendering upon task completion.
home.demoRemark: Purely static functional demo, no real Agent execution
home.architectureExplanationIntro: A frontend-native closed-loop architecture featuring Input, Reasoning, Dispatching, Execution, and Feedback:

architectureExplanationSteps.title: Conversation Portal (Top)
architectureExplanationSteps.desc: The user inputs natural language requests in the AI chatbox, initiating the intelligent decision-making chain.

architectureExplanationSteps.title: On-Device LLM (Central Brain)
architectureExplanationSteps.desc: Serves as the central hub for reasoning and dynamically matching the most relevant skills/tools.

architectureExplanationSteps.title: Skill Layer (Left WebSkill)
architectureExplanationSteps.desc: Contains declarative skill contracts and schema validation rules that define what requirements are needed for task execution.

architectureExplanationSteps.title: Interaction Layer (Right Generative UI)
architectureExplanationSteps.desc: Renders elegant dynamic forms to collect missing parameters, and renders final execution outputs into beautiful graphical charts.

architectureExplanationSteps.title: Execution Layer (Bottom WebMCP)
architectureExplanationSteps.desc: The frontend-native executor that interacts with page DOM elements or issues backend network requests to do the heavy lifting.

origin.splitTitle: 1.1 Web AI Architecture Split: Cloud AI vs Local AI
origin.splitDesc: Cloud AI (like ChatGPT) cannot interact closely with users' local environments (local files, LAN). Local AI (running models locally) faces hardware limitations. We need an architecture that uses Cloud AI for reasoning while executing actions safely in the local browser environment.
origin.mcpDesc: Anthropic's MCP (Model Context Protocol) standardized how LLMs call external tools. However, traditional MCP architectures run locally via stdio or SSE, requiring users to install desktop clients and configure complex scripts. This creates a huge barrier for ordinary users.
origin.contextTitle: 1.3 LLM Core Pain Point: Context Explosion
origin.contextDesc: If we give an LLM 100 tools, the system prompt describing these tools will consume massive context tokens. This leads to high costs, slower response times, and 'Lost in the middle' hallucinations where the LLM misinterprets tool usage.

architecture.opfsDesc: We propose packaging Agent tools into 'Skills' (folders containing SKILL.md, index.js, package.json). We download these skills from the cloud and store them in the browser's OPFS (Origin Private File System). They run in isolated Web Workers, ensuring absolute security and privacy.
architecture.routingDesc: Instead of giving all skills to the LLM at once, we use a lightweight routing agent. When a user sends a prompt, the router searches the OPFS catalog and only loads the relevant SKILL.md into the context.
architecture.progressiveDesc: Even within a single complex skill, there might be many tools. WebSkill uses 'Progressive Disclosure'. The LLM first reads SKILL.md, and only when it decides to use a specific tool does it request the tool's detailed parameters (JSON Schema). This minimizes token usage.
architecture.dynamicDesc: For dynamic context (e.g., checking stock on a specific product page), the web page itself acts as an MCP Server via a MessageChannel. It registers page-specific tools. When the page is closed, the skill vanishes.
architecture.generativeDesc: When a tool requires parameters the user hasn't provided, WebSkill outputs a JSON Schema. The UIBridge intercepts this and renders a Generative UI form for the user to fill out, creating a seamless human-in-the-loop interaction.

standards.idlDesc: We propose a new Web IDL standard: adding `navigator.webskill` to the browser. It includes SkillDiscovery (discover, read, validate), MinimalRuntime (run), and SkillManager (install, uninstall).
standards.exampleDesc: Using WebSkill in a web app will be as simple as a few lines of JavaScript.
standards.sec31Desc: Adding `navigator.webskill` to Navigator. It is partitioned into SkillDiscovery, MinimalRuntime, and SkillManager, establishing clear contracts for directory and metadata structures.
standards.sec32Desc: To enforce strict schema validation inside sandboxes and power tracing across the execution lifecycle, the draft specifies interfaces like `ValidationReport`, `RuntimeRun`, `Artifact`, and `TraceEvent`.
standards.sec33Desc: Specifies the returned data format structure returned during dynamic skill installation, including verification hash integrity, plus simple Web API calling examples.
standards.readmeDesc: Demonstrates how to register WebMCP tools on the main thread, establish low-latency MessageChannel transports for Workers, and invoke standard tool execution.

implement.s1Desc: WebSkill is implemented strictly in accordance with the standard Agent Skills protocol. The directory contains SKILL.md for metadata and instructions, scripts/ for executable code, references/ for required resources, and assets/ for static resources and intermediate outputs.
implement.s2Desc: To prevent context explosion, WebSkill loads skills on-demand. It first loads SKILL.md metadata to discover available skills, then loads the full instruction body when a skill is selected, and finally loads scripts and resources only during execution.
implement.s2L1Desc: LLM only loads names and descriptions (Table of Contents)
implement.s2L2Desc: LLM loads full SKILL.md body upon skill selection
implement.s2L3Desc: Loads scripts and assets only during execution
implement.s3Desc: Script files must be .ts/.js, export a 'run' function, and provide an 'inputSchema' (via explicit JSON Schema, TS type inference, or JSDoc). The return value strictly adheres to the MCP standard format.
implement.s3Req2Desc: Must export an execution function named 'run'
implement.s4Desc: For context control, page-level skills are dynamically exposed. The Web page main thread acts as an MCP Server registering prompts and tools, communicating via MessageChannel to a Worker sub-thread acting as an MCP Client. Skills expire when the page is closed.
implement.s4WorkerDesc: Discovers and calls tools via Client SDK
implement.s5Desc: When missing parameters are detected, WebSkill outputs a JSON Schema. A Generative UI renderer (via UIBridge) renders an interactive form. The state transitions from IDLE to PARAMS_COLLECTED, AWAITING_USER, and back to EXECUTING.
implement.s5BridgeDesc: Decouples WebSkill from UI frameworks (React, Vercel AI SDK, OpenUI, etc.)
implement.s1Exp1: Core Instructions (SKILL.md): The brain of each skill. It utilizes YAML frontmatter for skill declaration and Markdown guides to instruct the LLM on execution details.
implement.s1Exp2: Execution Logic (scripts/): Contains actionable execution scripts. Running inside a secure Web sandbox, it natively supports standard .ts and .js files.
implement.s1Exp3: Static Dependencies (references/): Stores required config files or render templates needed during script execution, such as report-template.html or dashboard.json.
implement.s1Exp4: Assets & Artifacts (assets/): Manages static assets like custom logos and automatically stores intermediate data or output reports generated by the Agent during execution.
implement.s2Exp1: Top Layer (SKILL.md Metadata): Initially, the LLM only loads names and descriptions (like an index table) to map capabilities.
implement.s2Exp2: Middle Layer (Instruction Layer): Loads the full SKILL.md prompt body only upon target skill selection.
implement.s2Exp3: Bottom Layer (Resource Layer): Dynamically retrieves scripts and references assets only when invocation or read triggers.
implement.s2Exp4: This Progressive Disclosure mechanism slashes redundant context overhead, optimizes token consumption, and significantly boosts model inference speeds compared to monolithic loading.
implement.s3Exp1: Strictly limited to standard .ts and .js files native to the web runtime.
implement.s3Exp2: Scripts must export a primary executable entry point named 'run'.
implement.s3Exp3: Must specify an 'inputSchema' so the AI can perform parameter synthesis and validation.
implement.s3Method1Desc: Directly define a standard JSON Schema object, ideal for complex, multi-layered parameter validation.
implement.s3Method2Desc: Simply write a clean TypeScript interface, and the compiler automatically derives the correct Schema contract.
implement.s3Method3Desc: Use standard JSDoc comments to document parameter types, allowing JavaScript scripts to also output typed schemas.
implement.s3Ret1: MCP Output Specification: Execution results must strictly follow the standard MCP response data return format (containing the content array). This ensures pipeline-style composability and fits seamlessly into post-execution lifecycles.
implement.s3Ret2: Hybrid MCP tools support: WebSkill supports dual-nature MCP runtimes: custom TypeScript-based servers mapped as 'endpoint:toolName', and experimental built-in browser APIs exposed as 'mcp#toolName'.
implement.s4Exp1: Dynamic Page-Level Skill Declaration: Persistent skills stored in OPFS are powerful, but as their numbers grow, even progressive disclosure can saturate the LLM's context. Page-Level Dynamic Skills offer the ultimate remedy. For instance, when a user lands on a product detail page, the AI instantly gains a transient skill to query that specific inventory. Once the page is closed, the skill evaporates automatically, keeping the context lightweight and perfectly scoped.
implement.s4Exp2: An elegant orchestration powered by standard MCP protocols:
implement.s4Exp3: Main Thread (MCP Server): The Main Thread acts as an MCP Server. Using standard MCP SDKs, it registers dynamic prompts via registerPrompt, script actions via registerTool, and page states via registerResource.
implement.s4Exp4: Lightweight Pipeline (MessageChannel): A MessageChannel Transport is established, enabling secure, low-latency communication across the main browser thread and the worker pool.
implement.s4Exp5: Worker Sub-thread (MCP Client): The Worker Sub-thread initializes an MCP Client, instantly discovering the main thread's page-level tools and invoking them dynamically.
implement.s4Exp6: When the user closes the web page, these dynamic skills disappear instantly with page destruction.
implement.s5Exp1: Generative UI (Generative UI): Fills the final puzzle piece of human-AI collaboration in the WebSkill workflow. When missing inputs are detected, the LLM outputs a rich schema instead of asking dry questions.
implement.s5Exp2: Dynamic Form Rendering: The UI bridge intercepts the Schema and renders a structured interactive form in the current conversation stream, turning user input into simple clicks, drags, or inputs.
implement.s5Exp3: WebSkill transitions seamlessly: IDLE ➔ PARAMS_COLLECTED (missing params detected) ➔ AWAITING_USER (execution paused, form rendered) ➔ COMPLETED (user submits) ➔ EXECUTING (continues execution with rich inputs).
implement.s5Exp4: Adaptive Bridge (UIBridge): UIBridge establishes a framework-agnostic boundary. Whether backed by Vercel AI SDK, React Schema Form, or OpenUI, components plug in as adapters, separating core execution logic from visual layers.
